subject: Physics
title: Schrödinger's Cat
description: Learn Schrödinger's Cat in one minute.

hook.text: Schrödinger imagined a cat in a box that's both alive and dead at the same time—until you look.

buildup.text: A radioactive atom has a 50% chance of decaying. If it decays, poison kills the cat. If not, the cat lives.

discovery.text: Quantum mechanics says the atom is both decayed and not decayed until observed. So the cat is both alive and dead.

twist.text: Schrödinger created this to show how absurd quantum superposition sounds when applied to everyday objects.

climax.text: The thought experiment remains physics' most famous paradox. It highlights the measurement problem in quantum mechanics.

punchline.text: Dead and alive until observed. The cat that broke common sense.

quiz.question: What was Schrödinger trying to demonstrate with his cat thought experiment?
options: The absurdity of applying quantum superposition to everyday objects
That radioactive decay can be predicted exactly
